titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner
Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «لِلدَّابَّةِ عَلَى صَاحِبِهَا خِصَالٌ يَبْدَأُ بِعَلْفِهَا إِذَا نَزَلَ وَ يَعْرِضُ عَلَيْهَا اَلْمَاءَ إِذَا مَرَّ بِهِ وَ لاَ يَضْرِبُ وَجْهَهَا فَإِنَّهَا تُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهَا وَ لاَ يَقِفُ عَلَى ظَهْرِهَا إِلاَّ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ لاَ يُحَمِّلُهَا فَوْقَ طَاقَتِهَا وَ لاَ يُكَلِّفُهَا مِنَ اَلْمَشْيِ إِلاَّ مَا تُطِيقُ».
arabic.text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «لِلدَّابَّةِ عَلَى صَاحِبِهَا خِصَالٌ يَبْدَأُ بِعَلْفِهَا إِذَا نَزَلَ وَ يَعْرِضُ عَلَيْهَا اَلْمَاءَ إِذَا مَرَّ بِهِ وَ لاَ يَضْرِبُ وَجْهَهَا فَإِنَّهَا تُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهَا وَ لاَ يَقِفُ عَلَى ظَهْرِهَا إِلاَّ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ لاَ يُحَمِّلُهَا فَوْقَ طَاقَتِهَا وَ لاَ يُكَلِّفُهَا مِنَ اَلْمَشْيِ إِلاَّ مَا تُطِيقُ».

segments:
Hadith.2465 - Isma‘il ibn Abi Ziyad narrated with his chain of transmission that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"A mount has certain rights upon its owner:
-	Begin by feeding it when you dismount.
-	Offer it water when you pass by a source.
-	Do not strike its face, for it glorifies its Lord (azj) with praise.
-	Do not remain seated on its back except in the way of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious.
-	Do not burden it beyond its capacity.
Do not compel it to walk more than it can endure."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2465 - Isma‘il ibn Abi Ziyad narrated with his chain of transmission that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"A mount has certain rights upon its owner:
-	Begin by feeding it when you dismount.
-	Offer it water when you pass by a source.
-	Do not strike its face, for it glorifies its Lord (azj) with praise.
-	Do not remain seated on its back except in the way of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious.
-	Do not burden it beyond its capacity.
Do not compel it to walk more than it can endure."

narratorChain.raw: 2465 - رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ بِإِسْنَادِهِ قَالَ

parts.text: 2465 - رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ بِإِسْنَادِهِ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: 2466 - وَ سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : مَتَى أَضْرِبُ دَابَّتِي تَحْتِي قَالَ «إِذَا لَمْ تَمْشِ تَحْتَكَ كَمَشْيِهَا إِلَى مِذْوَدِهَا».
arabic.text: 2466 - وَ سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : مَتَى أَضْرِبُ دَابَّتِي تَحْتِي قَالَ «إِذَا لَمْ تَمْشِ تَحْتَكَ كَمَشْيِهَا إِلَى مِذْوَدِهَا».

segments:
Hadith.2466 - A man asked Abu Abdullah (as): "When is it permissible for me to strike the animal I am riding?"
Imam (as) replied: "When it does not walk under you as it would walk toward its feeding trough."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2466 - A man asked Abu Abdullah (as): "When is it permissible for me to strike the animal I am riding?"
Imam (as) replied: "When it does not walk under you as it would walk toward its feeding trough."

titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: 2467 - وَ رُوِيَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «اِضْرِبُوهَا عَلَى اَلْعِثَارِ وَ لاَ تَضْرِبُوهَا عَلَى اَلنِّفَارِ فَإِنَّهَا تَرَى مَا لاَ تَرَوْنَ ».
arabic.text: 2467 - وَ رُوِيَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «اِضْرِبُوهَا عَلَى اَلْعِثَارِ وَ لاَ تَضْرِبُوهَا عَلَى اَلنِّفَارِ فَإِنَّهَا تَرَى مَا لاَ تَرَوْنَ ».

segments:
Hadith.2467 - It is narrated that Imam (as) said:
"Strike them for stumbling, but do not strike them for shying away, for they see what you do not see."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2467 - It is narrated that Imam (as) said:
"Strike them for stumbling, but do not strike them for shying away, for they see what you do not see."

titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: 2468 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِذَا عَثَرَتِ اَلدَّابَّةُ تَحْتَ اَلرَّجُلِ فَقَالَ لَهَا تَعَسْتِ تَقُولُ تَعَسَ أَعْصَانَا لِلرَّبِّ ».
arabic.text: 2468 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِذَا عَثَرَتِ اَلدَّابَّةُ تَحْتَ اَلرَّجُلِ فَقَالَ لَهَا تَعَسْتِ تَقُولُ تَعَسَ أَعْصَانَا لِلرَّبِّ ».

segments:
Hadith.2468 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"If a mount stumbles under its rider and the rider says to it, 'May you perish!' it responds, 'Perished is the one who disobeys our Lord (azj) the most.'"
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2468 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"If a mount stumbles under its rider and the rider says to it, 'May you perish!' it responds, 'Perished is the one who disobeys our Lord (azj) the most.'"

titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: 2469 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «فِي اَلدَّوَابِّ لاَ تَضْرِبُوا اَلْوُجُوهَ وَ لاَ تَلْعَنُوهَا فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَعَنَ لاَعِنَهَا » وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ «لاَ تُقَبِّحُوا اَلْوُجُوهَ.
arabic.text: 2469 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «فِي اَلدَّوَابِّ لاَ تَضْرِبُوا اَلْوُجُوهَ وَ لاَ تَلْعَنُوهَا فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَعَنَ لاَعِنَهَا » وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ «لاَ تُقَبِّحُوا اَلْوُجُوهَ.

segments:
Hadith.2469 - Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Regarding animals: Do not strike their faces and do not curse them, for Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, curses the one who curses them."
In another narration, Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Do not insult their faces."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2469 - Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Regarding animals: Do not strike their faces and do not curse them, for Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, curses the one who curses them."
In another narration, Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Do not insult their faces."

titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: 2470 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِنَّ اَلدَّوَابَّ إِذَا لُعِنَتْ لَزِمَتْهَا اَللَّعْنَةُ.
arabic.text: 2470 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِنَّ اَلدَّوَابَّ إِذَا لُعِنَتْ لَزِمَتْهَا اَللَّعْنَةُ.

segments:
Hadith.2470 - The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"When animals are cursed, the curse clings to them."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2470 - The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"When animals are cursed, the curse clings to them."

titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: 2471 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «لاَ تَتَوَرَّكُوا عَلَى اَلدَّوَابِّ وَ لاَ تَتَّخِذُوا ظُهُورَهَا مَجَالِسَ».
arabic.text: 2471 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «لاَ تَتَوَرَّكُوا عَلَى اَلدَّوَابِّ وَ لاَ تَتَّخِذُوا ظُهُورَهَا مَجَالِسَ».

segments:
Hadith.2471 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"Do not sit sideways on animals, and do not make their backs your seats."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2471 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"Do not sit sideways on animals, and do not make their backs your seats."

titles.en: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner

segments: 2472 - وَ قَالَ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ حُرْمَةٌ وَ حُرْمَةُ اَلْبَهَائِمِ فِي وُجُوهِهَا ».
arabic.text: 2472 - وَ قَالَ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ حُرْمَةٌ وَ حُرْمَةُ اَلْبَهَائِمِ فِي وُجُوهِهَا ».

segments:
Hadith.2472 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)  said:
"Everything has a sanctity, and the sanctity of animals lies in their faces."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.2472 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)  said:
"Everything has a sanctity, and the sanctity of animals lies in their faces."
